dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, I need someone I can trust to keep quiet. What I'm about to show you would get me run out of town.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: After the last raid, I found one of the {enemy_type} wounded and left behind by its own kind. I should have finished it off. Instead, I bandaged it. It looked at me, really looked at me, and I couldn't do it. Now it's healing, and I need {quest_item} from {location_hint} to treat the infection. {target_npc} would never understand.
dialogueChunks.outro: I know what they are and what they've done to us. But the {quest_stakes} {quest_stakes_is} at the heart of this. {quest_origin} changed everything I thought I knew. {quest_reward_hint} might come of showing mercy.

playerResponses.text: Mercy takes more courage than a killing blow. I'll help.
This is dangerous and foolish. But I'll get what you need.
You're harboring an enemy. I want no part of this.

dialogueChunks.intro: What I'm about to tell you will sound insane, {player_name}. But hear me out before you decide I've lost my mind.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The {enemy_type} we've been fighting for months aren't our biggest problem anymore. The {quest_threat} {quest_threat_has} moved into the area, and it's hunting them as brutally as it hunts us. One of their scouts approached {target_npc} unarmed and left behind {quest_item} as what I can only describe as a peace offering. {target_npc} thinks they want to fight the {quest_threat} together.
dialogueChunks.outro: If it's real, we could survive something neither side can face alone. If it's a trap, we lose everything. The {quest_focus} demands someone investigate. {quest_reward_hint} could follow.

playerResponses.text: An alliance against a common enemy? I'll investigate their offer.
Could be a trap, could be genuine. Either way, I want to know the truth.
Trusting the enemy is how settlements fall. Count me out.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, I found something after the last battle that changed everything I thought I knew about the {enemy_type}.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: When our guards drove the {enemy_type} back, they left behind a young one. Not a warrior, not a threat: just a small, frightened creature hiding in the wreckage. I've been feeding it and keeping it hidden, but {target_npc} is getting suspicious. The little one needs {quest_item} that I can't get without raising questions. There's a supply {location_hint}, but the roads are crawling with {enemy_type} looking for their lost kin.
dialogueChunks.outro: I can't hand it over to be killed, and I can't keep hiding it forever. The {quest_stakes} depends on what we do next. {quest_origin} started all of this.

playerResponses.text: A child is a child, regardless of what it grows into. I'll help.
You're risking a lot for a creature that would grow up to raid us. But fine.
That thing's parents tried to kill us. It doesn't belong here.
